UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents a user's profile, including personal details, skill levels, and preferences.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

name.description: The display name of the player.

playingPreferences.description: A list of the player's preferred match types or play styles (e.g., 'Doubles', 'Singles', 'Aggressive', 'Defensive').

profileImageUrl.description: URL to the player's profile picture.

dateJoined.description: Timestamp when the user registered for the app.

lastActive.description: Timestamp of the user's last activity in the app.

favoriteCourtIds.description: References to Courts that this user has marked as favorites. (Relationship: UserProfile N:N Court)

Court.title: Court
Court.description: Represents a badminton court available for queuing or booking.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Court entity.

name.description: The name of the court (e.g., 'Court 1', 'Main Hall Court B').

location.description: The physical address or a descriptive location of the court.

description.description: Additional descriptive details about the court.

hourlyRate.description: The cost to book the court per hour.

imageUrl.description: URL to an image illustrating the court.

openingTime.description: The daily opening time of the court (e.g., '08:00').

closingTime.description: The daily closing time of the court (e.g., '22:00').

Queue.title: Queue
Queue.description: Represents a dynamic waiting list for a specific court.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Queue entity.

courtId.description: Reference to the Court this queue is for. (Relationship: Court 1:N Queue)

name.description: A descriptive name for the queue (e.g., 'Court 1 Morning Doubles Queue').

status.description: The current operational status of the queue (e.g., 'Active', 'Closed', 'Paused').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the queue was created.

estimatedWaitTimeMinutes.description: The estimated time in minutes for a player to get a turn in this queue.

QueueEntry.title: QueueEntry
QueueEntry.description: Represents a player's entry into a specific court queue.

id.description: Unique identifier for the QueueEntry entity.

queueId.description: Reference to the Queue this entry belongs to. (Relationship: Queue 1:N QueueEntry)

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who joined the queue. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N QueueEntry)

joinTime.description: Timestamp when the user joined the queue.

leaveTime.description: Timestamp when the user left the queue, if applicable (can be null if still active).

status.description: The current status of the player within the queue (e.g., 'Waiting', 'Playing', 'Left', 'Skipped').

Match.title: Match
Match.description: Represents a badminton match, whether suggested by AI or actively played.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Match entity.

courtId.description: Reference to the Court where the match is played. (Relationship: Court 1:N Match)

queueId.description: Optional reference to the Queue from which this match was formed. (Relationship: Queue 1:N Match)

startTime.description: The actual or planned start time of the match.

endTime.description: The actual or planned end time of the match (can be null if not yet completed).

status.description: The current status of the match (e.g., 'Suggested', 'Scheduled', 'In Progress', 'Completed', 'Cancelled').

playerIds.description: References to all UserProfiles participating in this match. (Relationship: UserProfile N:N Match)

suggestedByAI.description: Indicates if this match pairing was suggested by the AI-powered tool.

score.description: The final score of the match, if completed (e.g., '21-15, 21-18').

winningPlayerIds.description: References to UserProfiles in the winning team or side, if applicable. (Relationship: UserProfile N:N Match)

Booking.title: Booking
Booking.description: Represents a direct reservation of a court for a specific time slot.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Booking entity.

courtId.description: Reference to the Court that has been booked. (Relationship: Court 1:N Booking)

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile who made the booking. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N Booking)

startTime.description: The exact start time of the booking.

endTime.description: The exact end time of the booking.

status.description: The current status of the booking (e.g., 'Confirmed', 'Pending', 'Cancelled', 'Completed').

bookedAt.description: Timestamp when the booking was created.

totalCost.description: The total cost associated with this booking.

MatchHistoryEntry.title: MatchHistoryEntry
MatchHistoryEntry.description: Represents a user's specific participation and outcome in a past match, allowing for personalized feedback.

id.description: Unique identifier for the MatchHistoryEntry entity.

userId.description: Reference to the UserProfile this history entry belongs to. (Relationship: UserProfile 1:N MatchHistoryEntry)

matchId.description: Reference to the Match this history entry pertains to. (Relationship: Match 1:N MatchHistoryEntry)

outcome.description: The user's specific outcome in the match (e.g., 'Win', 'Loss', 'Draw').

performanceRating.description: A rating of the user's performance in this specific match (e.g., 1-5 stars).

notes.description: Any personal notes or reflections the user has about this match.

definition.description: Stores individual user profiles. Access is restricted to the user whose 'userId' matches 'request.auth.uid'. This path uses path-based ownership for authorization.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier (Firebase Auth UID) of the user whose profile is being stored or accessed.

definition.description: Stores public information about badminton courts. Generally publicly readable for discovery. Write access is restricted to system administrators.

params.name: courtId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific court.

definition.description: Stores information about active queues for specific courts. Includes 'courtId'. Publicly readable for real-time status updates. Write access restricted to system administrators.

params.name: queueId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific court queue.

definition.description: Represents a user's entry into a specific queue. Includes 'userId' for authorization independence, allowing users to manage their own entries (read, update, delete) without parent lookups. Queue administrators can read all entries within 'their' queues.

params.name: queueId
params.description: The unique identifier of the queue this entry belongs to.

params.name: queueEntryId
params.description: The unique identifier for this specific queue entry.

definition.description: Stores details about badminton matches. Includes 'playerIds' and 'courtId'. Player-participants (`request.auth.uid` in `playerIds`) can read/update their match details. Public can read completed matches. This includes denormalized 'playerIds' for authorization independence.

params.name: matchId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific match.

definition.description: Represents a direct court reservation. Includes 'userId' and 'courtId'. Users can only read/write their own bookings, enabled by denormalized 'userId'. System administrators can manage bookings for specific 'courtId's.

params.name: bookingId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific court booking.

definition.description: Stores individual user-specific match history entries. Access is strictly restricted to the user whose 'userId' matches 'request.auth.uid'. This path uses path-based ownership for authorization.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier (Firebase Auth UID) of the user this match history entry belongs to.

params.name: matchHistoryEntryId
params.description: The unique identifier for this specific match history entry.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore data structure for ShuttleQueue prioritizes Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and consistent Access Modeling to ensure robust, debuggable security rules and efficient queries. The core principle is to avoid `get()` calls in security rules by denormalizing authorization-relevant data directly into the documents that require access control. This strategy also inherently supports Queryable Access Patterns (QAPs) by allowing rule-enforced queries (e.g., `where('userId', '==', request.auth.uid)`).

**Authorization Independence via Denormalization:**
*   **UserProfile:** Each user's profile is stored at `/userProfiles/{userId}`, where `{userId}` is the Firebase Authentication UID. Access is strictly path-based, meaning `request.auth.uid` must match `{userId}` for read/write operations. This makes authorization atomic and independent of other documents.
*   **QueueEntry:** Documents in `/queues/{queueId}/queueEntries/{queueEntryId}` contain the `userId` of the participant. A user can read/write their own `QueueEntry` by verifying `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId` without performing a `get()` on `/userProfiles/{userId}`. This enables secure user-specific operations and QAPs for listing a user's current queue entries.
*   **Match:** Documents in `/matches/{matchId}` contain a `playerIds` array. Participants can access a match if `request.auth.uid` is present in `resource.data.playerIds`. This denormalization eliminates the need to `get()` user profiles or other related data for authorization, facilitating efficient queries for matches a user is involved in.
*   **Booking:** Documents in `/bookings/{bookingId}` include both `userId` and `courtId`. A user can manage their booking if `request.auth.uid == resource.data.userId`. Court administrators (whose role is defined externally, see below) can manage bookings for a specific `courtId`. This supports QAPs for users to view their own bookings and for administrators to view bookings per court.
*   **MatchHistoryEntry:** Stored under `/userProfiles/{userId}/matchHistory/{matchHistoryEntryId}`, its ownership is strictly defined by the path. `request.auth.uid` must match `{userId}` for access, ensuring privacy and atomic authorization.

**Queryable Access Patterns (QAPs) and Structural Segregation:**
*   **Publicly Readable Collections:** `/courts/{courtId}` and `/queues/{queueId}` are designed to be publicly readable, allowing users to discover available courts and view real-time queue statuses without authentication. Write access to these collections is restricted to system administrators, ensuring data integrity. This segregation ensures a homogeneous security posture.
*   **User-Specific Data:** `UserProfile` and `MatchHistoryEntry` are stored in user-specific subcollections (`/userProfiles/{userId}/...`), which naturally enforces ownership and makes `list` operations simple for the authenticated user (`request.auth.uid`).
*   **Collaborative/Shared Data:** `Match`, `QueueEntry`, and `Booking` are top-level collections or subcollections under shared entities (`/queues/{queueId}/queueEntries`). By denormalizing `userId` or `playerIds`, these collections can be securely queried (`list`) by users for their relevant entries, and by administrators for broader oversight, all while respecting security rules and avoiding filtering data on the client side.

**Database Access Control (DBAC):**
*   For system-wide administrative roles, a dedicated top-level collection like `/admin_roles/{adminId}` would be used. The mere existence of a document at `/admin_roles/$(request.auth.uid)` grants administrative privileges. This follows the "Existence over Content" principle and avoids custom claims, relying solely on `request.auth.uid` and database lookups in rules. While not represented as a formal 'entity' in the provided schema, this collection is crucial for securing administrative actions (e.g., creating/updating courts and queues).
